repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Refactor #37977
Fix #38667
Fix #38630

## Problem
After #37977, the Windows builtin TUI startup path began treating every normal `nvim` launch like a detached-server case. That fixed `:detach` / `:restart`, but it also pulled ordinary startup into console-handling logic that was only meant for those persistence flows, causing two regressions:

1. The embedded server was started detached and allocated a hidden console on startup, so a hidden `conhost.exe` appeared on every launch and `CONOUT$` resolved to that hidden console instead of the real terminal. As a result, `io.stdout:write()` rendering such as SIXEL and Kitty image output disappeared. #38667

2. Because normal startup was still spawned as a detached Windows process, terminal jobs no longer behaved like they did in a normal attached console session. In particular, `Ctrl-C` in `:terminal` stopped interrupting foreground programs on Windows. #38630

## Solution
Keep the detached Windows builtin-TUI startup model introduced for `:detach` / `:restart`, but narrow the console-handling fallout it caused.

1. Preserve detached startup for the embedded server, so the existing Windows `:detach` and `:restart` behavior continues to work.

2. Restore useful console behavior on top of that detached model:
   - top-level builtin TUI startup may borrow the parent console so `CONOUT$` resolves to the real terminal, preserving visible `io.stdout:write()` output such as SIXEL and Kitty image rendering
   - `:restart` still allocates a hidden private console for the replacement server
   - `:detach` still swaps the running server to a hidden private console after the UI/channel is closed

3. After the detached embedded server acquires a console, restore normal `Ctrl-C` handling so terminal jobs can inherit it again on Windows.

> **Note:** skipped 'killing TUI process without :preserve #22096' test on Windows

comment_data.body: I mean this part should be moved to `nvim__chan_set_detach()`.
Yeah, i get it, i am not so sure about it yet i thought that the test failed because rpc died or something
but anyway probably calling `nvim__chan_set_detach `from `ex_detach `is still kinda falls under refactor commit
Oh maybe the problem is a bit more complicated.

comment_regular_data.body:
Test failure:
```
FAILED   test/functional/terminal/tui_spec.lua @ 4263: TUI client :restart works when connecting to remote instance (with its own TUI)
test/functional/terminal\tui_spec.lua:4268: Row 2 did not match.
Expected:
  |^                                                  |
  |*{100:~                                                 }|
  |*{100:~                                                 }|
  |*{100:~                                                 }|
  |*{3:[No Name]                                         }|
  |                                                  |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
Actual:
  |^                                                  |
  |*[Process exited 0]                                |
  |*                                                  |
  |*                                                  |
  |*                                                  |
  |                                                  |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
```
Perhaps this should be moved to `nvim__chan_set_detach()`, and `ex_detach()` should call that API.

Well, seems like I will add later an env something like in ef00154 so `AttachConsole(ATTACH_PARENT_PROCESS)` would attach to the parent's ConPTY and allow `io.stdout:write()` on first run, but on `:restart` it will just skip it through this env, so after `:restart` it won't work, seems pretty simple, but need to figure out some other issues like in ef00154. Though, I don't find it necessary to fix this fully just to support rendering on windows-only,  guess https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37914 just wait for this one
Or perhaps having `:restart` reattach all UIs will solve this?

> Or perhaps having `:restart` reattach all UIs will solve this?

You mean io.stdout:write() writing to hidden console? I fell like they different layers reattaching more ui's doesn't change where `$CONOUT` points to

regarding test it fails because it's nvim-inside-nvim-terminal case, so we see  `[Process exited 0]`  because the new server's console ConPTY is destroyed when the old server exits, sending CTRL_CLOSE_EVENT that kills the new process. which is attached because of `AttachConsole(ATTACH_PARENT_PROCESS))`, as i mentioned via env we prob skip this part and allocate so new nvim survivives via calling `AllocConsole()`, but it does not allow `io.stdout.write()` then. Well i don't know if there is any smart solution to this

> so we see  `[Process exited 0]`  because the new server's console ConPTY is destroyed when the old server exits

I was speculating that this happens because the old client exits, not because the old server exits. So having the old client also reattach will solve this problem.

Well this looks better now, but as it is after running `:restart/:detach` because of swap to make server survive the `io.stdout:write()` won't work. Way to go about it is forward writes to client before they go to hidden console? - maybe in another pr, though how necessary is this for https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37914

Hmm, the second commit is a bit strange. Since it only fixes the "unreadable swapfile when closing parent terminal" problem for a nested Nvim, does the problem still happen when running Nvim in another terminal? If that's the case, I think it's better to just adjust the swapfile test for now, and leave the fix for another PR.

labels.description: changes that are not features or bugfixes
labels.name: refactor

title: fix(channel): fix Windows console regressions from #37977
